taxonomy: Gurltia meyeri is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, and it is classified as a member of the genus Gurltia. It is a part of the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. The plant's classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Gurltia meyeri is native to the Himalayan region of Asia and is often found growing in mountainous areas. The plant's classification is a reflection of its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the same family and genus.

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat gastrointestinal problems and skin infections.
